session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8842 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll execute the skill by fetching the PR feedback data and classifying it.
session.tool_result:
{
  "success": true,
  "pr_number": 8842,
  "pr_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8842",
  "pr_title": "Consolidate documentation from 14 erk-learn plans",
  "review_threads": [
    {
      "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yu0E0",
      "path": "docs/learned/architecture/git-plumbing-patterns.md",
      "line": 89,
      "is_outdated": false,
      "comments": [
        {
          "author": "github-actions",
          "body": "**Audit PR Docs**: Inaccurate claim detected.

Claim: "After committing, if the branch is checked out it runs `git checkout HEAD --` on the committed `impl_context_paths` via `run_subprocess_with_context()` to bring the index back in sync."
Reality: The actual implementation (lines 136-141) writes the committed files directly to disk (`abs_path.write_text()`) and stages them via `git.commit.stage_files()`. No `git checkout HEAD --` or `run_subprocess_with_context()` is used.

Source: `src/erk/cli/commands/exec/scripts/incremental_dispatch.py:135-141`

Suggested fix: Update the description to reflect the actual implementation: "After committing, if the branch is checked out it writes the impl-context files directly to the worktree path and stages them via `stage_files()`."",
          "created_at": "2026-03-07T04:33:45Z"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "discussion_comments": [
    {
      "id": 4012464337,
      "author": "schrockn",
      "body": "Plan Queued for Implementation
